compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps
1. Merge a change to infra/scripts/observability.sh (e.g. change 491's root_disk_used_percent section) and let autodeploy roll /opt/rebar forward.
2. autodeploy materialises compose/config but never re-runs infra/scripts/install-observability.sh, which is the only thing that copies observability.sh to /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh (the path the systemd timer executes).
3. The timer keeps running the stale copy.

**Expected:** landing an observability change on main reaches the box's running probe on the next deploy. **Actual:** /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh was a Jun 30 copy (7,196 bytes) while /opt/rebar held 16,275 bytes — the deploy_errors (4d), merge-change (4c) and root-disk (2b) metric sections silently never ran in prod. Found 2026-07-10 verifying ac14; remediated one-off by re-running install-observability.sh via SSM. This is the same drift class as incident 2731 (provisioning script not extended with the artifact it must manage), one layer up: the deploy loop does not own installer-materialized artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] autodeploy's materialise step refreshes /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh when infra/scripts/observability.sh changes (re-run install-observability.sh, or install the file directly like other materialised artifacts).
- [ ] A drift check (config-check or a test) fails when the materialised copy can go stale — i.e. the install path is derived from one source of truth, mirroring the b005 --print-volumes pattern.
- [ ] Evidence on the box post-deploy: /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh matches /opt/rebar/infra/scripts/observability.sh after an autodeploy run.
- [ ] File paths: infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/install-observability.sh.
compiled_state.title: autodeploy never refreshes installer-materialized host scripts — /usr/local/bin/rebar-observability.sh went 10 days stale
